text: Judge Cannon permanently blocks release of Jack Smith report - Politico

text: Nothing couldn’t wait to show off the Phone 4A - The Verge

text: Deep in Antarctic ice, these particles can answer basic questions about the universe - The Washington Post
fullText: A dense network of sensors is looking for the fleeting footprints of neutrinos, the most mysterious in the pantheon of known particles.

text: Rob Jetten sworn in as youngest-ever Dutch prime minister - DW.com
fullText: The 38-year-old centrist is also the Netherlands' first openly gay prime minister. His minority coalition is set to be put to the test in an already  fractured political landscape.

text: Gilead to acquire cancer therapy developer Arcellx for up to $7.8 billion - CNBC
fullText: Gilead Sciences will pay as much as $7.8 billion to acquire partner Arcellx in ​its largest deal since 2020, the ​biopharma company said on ​Monday.

text: New research suggests some cases of autism could be preventable - The Washington Post
fullText: Some research suggests that avoiding environmental exposures, especially pre-conception, may help. Influencers branded the idea “trimester zero,” but the science is in early stages.

text: Say Goodbye to the Undersea Cable That Made the Global Internet Possible - WIRED
fullText: History was unmade last year, as engineers began the massive project of ripping the first-ever transoceanic fiber-optic cable from the ocean floor. Just don’t mention sharks.

text: The owners want to close this Colorado coal plant. The Trump administration says no - NPR
fullText: The Trump administration has ordered several coal plants to keep operating past their planned retirement, part of a larger effort to boost the coal industry. Two Colorado utilities are pushing back.
